summary: coverage.unmodeled-surface falsely calls partially-governed settings.json wholly ungoverned while its hook segment is checked; narrow the finding to the ungoverned residue

edit.description: A manifest with a governed segment and an ungoverned residue is partially governed: settings.json's hooks.<Event> segment is checked (member_counts carries hook (3)) yet its unmodeled-surface message still reads `no kind governs it — temper checks none of its members`, a false claim. `governs` returns false for a segment kind (collection address not spanning the whole manifest) and `check` then emits the wholly-ungoverned message; narrow it so a partially-governed manifest names only the ungoverned residue (permissions, env), never claiming none of its members are checked. The `governs` doc comment's `keeps its finding until every segment is modeled` middle is the current false-claim source.

tests.asserts: a partially-governed settings.json (hooks segment checked, permissions/env residue ungoverned) yields an unmodeled-surface message that names the ungoverned residue and never claims `no kind governs it` or `temper checks none of its members`; a wholly-ungoverned manifest (.mcp.json with no mcp-server kind) still reports the full finding, and a wholly-governed one (.mcp.json under mcp-server) still retires it.

acceptance: A partially-governed manifest (settings.json with the hooks segment checked) yields a coverage.unmodeled-surface message stating only true things — it names the ungoverned residue and never claims `no kind governs it` or `temper checks none of its members`. `cargo run -- check .temper` no longer contradicts its own `hook (3)` coverage line.
notes: Live invariant-6 violation; not deferrable to MANIFEST-WRITE-COVERAGE-RETIRE, which retires the finding only once settings.json is FULLY represented — this fixes the intermediate partial-governance message NOW. mcp-server ship (1ffab8f) added spans_whole_manifest and retired .mcp.json's finding but left settings.json's segment case lying. Probe: cargo run -- check .temper shows hook (3) and 'checks none of its members' in one run. observed at 15a71ac; scoped at ff84346

summary: Manifest write side 1/5: SDK member constructors for hook/mcp-server registration members, erased into the emit payload as embedded-member facts

edit.description: Member-authoring constructors for hook and mcp-server registration members (instances, not the KindDefinitions already at 182/230): a hook keyed under hooks.<Event>, an mcp-server keyed by name under mcpServers.* — each carrying the transport/command/matcher fields the read face already surfaces.

Erase authored registration members into the emit payload's embedded-member facts (name, collection address, folded fields) — the same declaration-row shape the engine write face will consume. A projection is never mined for these (pipeline.md 'Emit'); they are carried from the composing program.

tests.asserts: an authored hook and an authored mcp-server member each erase into the payload as embedded-member collection facts — name-keyed at their collection address, transport/command fields folded — matching the fact shape a manifest write face reads back.

acceptance: The SDK can author a hook and an mcp-server registration member and emit erases each into the payload's embedded-member facts (name, collection address, fields), ready for the engine write face to render.
notes: Entry 1 of the MANIFEST-WRITE chain, re-scoped from the parked MANIFEST-WRITE-SIDE placeholder now phase 1 (MANIFEST-ADAPTER-READ/HOOK-KIND/MCP-SERVER-KIND) fully shipped. No upstream: the SDK produces the facts before the engine consumes them. builtins.ts already exports the hook/mcpServer KindDefinitions (182/230). scoped at 5f27db2

summary: Manifest write side 2/5: canonical whole-manifest write face (json_manifest.rs) + engine.rs routing represented manifests through it; json_splice stays the unrepresented path

edit.description: Write face extending the phase-1 read face: canonical whole-manifest regeneration — declared collection-address order then the opaque residue, LF — under the container member's provenance row. Registration members carry no lock rows of their own (0012).

Emit wires the represented-manifest projection: a container member whose segment is a represented manifest regenerates through the general write face, not json_splice (which stays the unrepresented-manifest path).

tests.asserts: a represented manifest (a hooks segment plus an opaque residue) regenerates whole via the write face — declared collection-address order then the residue, LF — and a double-emit is byte-identical; an unrepresented manifest still routes through json_splice unchanged.

acceptance: A represented JSON manifest regenerates whole via json_manifest.rs's write face (declared order then residue, LF, under the container's provenance row); emit routes represented manifests there and leaves unrepresented ones on json_splice.
notes: Entry 2. Consumes entry 1's payload facts. json_splice is today the sole manifest edit path (install.rs); this adds the represented-manifest write face beside it, not replacing it. Double-emit byte-check per pipeline.md 'Emit'. scoped at 5f27db2

summary: Manifest write side 3/5: route bundle.rs's bespoke serde_json manifest writes through the canonical write face — consolidation, not a second encoder

edit.description: Convert the three bespoke serde_json manifest builders — plugin_manifest/marketplace_manifest/hooks_manifest and write_json (bundle.rs:206/218/236/253) — to general-write instances of the canonical manifest write (0015's named consequence). One encoder, no second serde_json path.

tests.asserts: the plugin.json/marketplace.json/hooks.json bundle.rs emits are byte-identical before and after routing through the canonical write, and are produced by the one write surface — no second serde_json encoder remains.

acceptance: bundle.rs's plugin.json/marketplace.json/hooks.json are written through the canonical manifest write face, not a bespoke serde_json path; one encoder, byte-identical output.
notes: Entry 3, consolidation per one-job-one-home. bundle.rs today builds three manifests via serde_json::to_string_pretty (bundle.rs:206/218/236/253). The canonical write face (entry 2) is the single home; this removes the duplicate encoder. scoped at 5f27db2

summary: Manifest write side 4/5: the PreToolUse guard checks a represented manifest's members against their contract, extending today's .claude/-projection-drift-only binding

per.section: The placements and their enforcement modes

edit.description: Guard coverage of represented manifests — the PreToolUse guard checks a represented manifest's members against their contract, following the author's declared enforcement mode (block/warn/note). Today the guard greps only for .claude/ projection paths (GUARD_MESSAGE plus the projection-path regex, install.rs ~117-126).

tests.asserts: the PreToolUse guard flags a represented manifest whose member violates its contract, at the author's declared enforcement mode, and passes a conforming one; the existing .claude/ projection-drift binding is unaffected.

acceptance: The PreToolUse guard checks a represented manifest's members against contract, following the author's declared enforcement mode (block/warn/note) — not only .claude/ projection drift.
notes: Entry 4. install.rs's guard today greps for .claude/ projection paths (GUARD_MESSAGE + the projection regex, ~117-126); this adds represented-manifest member coverage. The three enforcement values (block/warn/note) split by where the finding goes, per distribution.md. scoped at 5f27db2

summary: Manifest write side 5/5: fully retire settings.json's coverage.unmodeled-surface once represented — its permissions/env residue then covered

edit.description: A fully-represented manifest — every segment modeled, no ungoverned residue — no longer reports coverage.unmodeled-surface: settings.json's permissions/env residue is covered once represented, retiring the finding COVERAGE-PARTIAL-GOVERNANCE narrowed to that residue and HOOK-KIND began for the hooks segment.

tests.asserts: a fully-represented settings.json (hooks plus permissions/env all modeled) reports no coverage.unmodeled-surface finding; a partially-governed one still names its residue (COVERAGE-PARTIAL-GOVERNANCE's case) and a wholly-ungoverned one still reports the full finding.

acceptance: Once settings.json is fully represented (hooks + permissions/env all modeled), its coverage.unmodeled-surface finding fully retires — no residue left to name.
notes: Entry 5. Builds on COVERAGE-PARTIAL-GOVERNANCE (ships first as head — narrows the finding to the residue) and needs representation possible (entries 1-2). blockedBy names the deep blocker MANIFEST-WRITE-EMIT-FACE; COVERAGE-PARTIAL-GOVERNANCE will have shipped by the time this opens. scoped at 5f27db2

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, public on npm — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP, 543f2fd); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Interim SDK releases ride 0.0.x — sdk/package.json is 0.0.6 (the unpublished 0.1.0 re-cut down at 55d7ee4; 0.1.0 waits for the v0.1 launch tag it gates). Re-verified live at 747f8cc: still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
